title: Helm Configuration
description: Configuration schema for helm - tmux session manager TUI

appearance.description: Terminal appearance mode - controls color adaptation for borders, selection, and scrollbar. Updated automatically by pick-theme. Ignored when a theme is set (the theme carries its own appearance).

theme.description: Black Atom theme key. When set, helm renders with that theme's palette; empty uses terminal-native colors (ANSI-16 + reverse video). Env override: HELM_THEME.

layout.description: Layout script name to apply when creating new sessions

enable_layouts.description: Enable layout scripts feature (when disabled, layouts won't be auto-applied)

git_status_enabled.description: Enable git status indicator (shows dirty/ahead/behind for repos)

project_dirs.description: Base directories for project picker (Ctrl+p). Supports multiple paths - all will be scanned.

project_depth.description: Scan depth for project directories (2 = owner/repo structure)

default_session_dir.description: Default directory for new sessions created with Ctrl+n

bookmarks.description: Quick-access session bookmarks (slots 1-9, maps to M-1 through M-9). Use 'helm tmux-bindings' to generate tmux keybindings. Note: Bookmarks are stored separately in ~/.config/black-atom/helm/bookmarks.yml.

path.description: Absolute or home-relative path to the project directory

dirty_walkthrough_command.description: Command to run on each dirty repo via 'helm repos dirty --walk'. Use `{}` as placeholder for the repo path, e.g. `lazygit -p {}`

ensure_cloned.description: Repositories to ensure are cloned (used by 'helm setup'). Supports plain URLs and objects with post_clone hooks. Wildcard patterns (org/*) expand via gh CLI.

post_clone.description: Shell command to run after cloning

git_providers.description: Maps git hosts to directory aliases for clone destination paths. Empty string = use path as-is. Omitted hosts use host/ as prefix, except github.com which defaults to no prefix (owner/repo). A leading ~ in paths (e.g. ~alice/project) is stripped for the local directory name.
